generatedSentence: Our protagonist, a retired professional jogger, now living in a peculiar residency overlooking an oasis by a lake, often spent his days in fluffy pyjamas, enjoying oatmeal and a single donut while a toucan watched. He possessed a benevolent, almost playful nature, his kinesics fluid during gentle exercise, even as his cat, with its long whiskers, watched him watering plants. His vacation days were spent volunteering at a kindergarten, making waffles and cookies for the nestling children, creating an immaculate and serene life. Yet, a terse, unilateral message arrived, hinting at jactitation within the underworld's elite; a fanatical group engaged in hacking old records. A former policeman, now a reluctant walker, he knew this opposition was brutal, having faced their unconscious tactics before. The message spoke of a warhead threat, a plausible but terrifying prospect that made his usual chowder suddenly unpalatable, stirring old ambitions to confront the looming shadow. The conflict escalated rapidly: a hijacking of a truck carrying packaged items of immense power was underway, orchestrated by shadowy couriers. He knew the location of their makeshift factory, a place of steam and high kilojoules, where trojans were deployed. He moved with the precision of jujutsu, carrying a holster and a single feather as a good luck charm. He scaled an ancient archway, the moon above a dazzling ember, preparing to confront the neanderthal tactics of the opposition, knowing this was his marathon. Afterward, the factory lay silent, the dishwasher running with blanks for the now-contained warhead. He felt a sense of closure, an impeachment of the underworld's reign. The jaguar figurine on his desk, a gift from a grateful saint, seemed to purr. He still had the grater and sink full of items from the earlier struggle, but the immediate injury was averted. He contemplated the versatility of his skills, like a narwhal navigating depths, before finally allowing himself the luxury of a long-overdue logoff and peace.
panelSentences: Our protagonist, a retired professional jogger, now living in a peculiar residency overlooking an oasis by a lake, often spent his days in fluffy pyjamas, enjoying oatmeal and a single donut while a toucan watched. He possessed a benevolent, almost playful nature, his kinesics fluid during gentle exercise, even as his cat, with its long whiskers, watched him watering plants. His vacation days were spent volunteering at a kindergarten, making waffles and cookies for the nestling children, creating an immaculate and serene life.

Yet, a terse, unilateral message arrived, hinting at jactitation within the underworld's elite; a fanatical group engaged in hacking old records. A former policeman, now a reluctant walker, he knew this opposition was brutal, having faced their unconscious tactics before. The message spoke of a warhead threat, a plausible but terrifying prospect that made his usual chowder suddenly unpalatable, stirring old ambitions to confront the looming shadow.

The conflict escalated rapidly: a hijacking of a truck carrying packaged items of immense power was underway, orchestrated by shadowy couriers. He knew the location of their makeshift factory, a place of steam and high kilojoules, where trojans were deployed. He moved with the precision of jujutsu, carrying a holster and a single feather as a good luck charm. He scaled an ancient archway, the moon above a dazzling ember, preparing to confront the neanderthal tactics of the opposition, knowing this was his marathon.

Afterward, the factory lay silent, the dishwasher running with blanks for the now-contained warhead. He felt a sense of closure, an impeachment of the underworld's reign. The jaguar figurine on his desk, a gift from a grateful saint, seemed to purr. He still had the grater and sink full of items from the earlier struggle, but the immediate injury was averted. He contemplated the versatility of his skills, like a narwhal navigating depths, before finally allowing himself the luxury of a long-overdue logoff and peace.
rawPanelSentences: Our protagonist, a retired professional **jogger**, now living in a peculiar **residency** overlooking an **oasis** by a **lake**, often spent his days in fluffy **pyjamas**, enjoying **oatmeal** and a single **donut** while a **toucan** watched. He possessed a **benevolent**, almost **playful** nature, his **kinesics** fluid during gentle **exercise**, even as his cat, with its long **whiskers**, watched him **watering** plants. His **vacation** days were spent volunteering at a **kindergarten**, making **waffles** and **cookies** for the **nestling** children, creating an **immaculate** and serene life.

Yet, a **terse**, **unilateral message** arrived, hinting at **jactitation** within the **underworld**'s **elite**; a **fanatical** group engaged in **hacking** old **records**. A former **policeman**, now a reluctant **walker**, he knew this **opposition** was **brutal**, having faced their **unconscious** tactics before. The **message** spoke of a **warhead** threat, a **plausible** but terrifying prospect that made his usual **chowder** suddenly unpalatable, stirring old **ambitions** to confront the looming shadow.

The conflict escalated rapidly: a **hijacking** of a **truck** carrying **packaged** **items** of immense **power** was underway, orchestrated by shadowy **couriers**. He knew the **location** of their makeshift **factory**, a place of **steam** and high **kilojoules**, where **trojans** were deployed. He moved with the precision of **jujutsu**, carrying a **holster** and a single **feather** as a good luck charm. He scaled an ancient **archway**, the **moon** above a dazzling ember, preparing to confront the **neanderthal** tactics of the **opposition**, knowing this was his **marathon**.

Afterward, the **factory** lay silent, the **dishwasher** running with **blanks** for the now-contained **warhead**. He felt a sense of closure, an **impeachment** of the **underworld**'s reign. The **jaguar** figurine on his desk, a gift from a grateful **saint**, seemed to purr. He still had the **grater** and **sink** full of items from the earlier struggle, but the immediate **injury** was averted. He contemplated the versatility of his skills, like a **narwhal** navigating depths, before finally allowing himself the luxury of a long-overdue **logoff** and peace.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A male protagonist, mid-30s, short black hair, wearing a sleek black armored jumpsuit with subtle glowing blue accents and a determined expression. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a tranquil, neon-lit oasis home, gently watering a bonsai tree, a futuristic cityscape visible through a large window, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist receives a holographic message, his expression shifting to grim determination, a close-up on his face with glowing message reflections. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, armed, in a dynamic action pose, scaling a futuristic archway against a backdrop of a neon-soaked industrial complex, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist standing victorious over the defeated remnants of a futuristic factory, a deactivated warhead in the background, a sense of calm after the storm, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: Bragging or boasting, especially in a false manner to another's detriment.
A false pretense of being married to somebody.
Extreme restlessness; tossing and turning in bed.
An appliance or room used to store food or other perishable items at temperatures below 0° Celsius (32° Fahrenheit).
The section of a refrigerator used to store food or other perishable items at a temperature below 0° Celsius (32° Fahrenheit).
(Parkinson's disease) A Parkinson's disease patient that experiences freezing of gait (FOG) episodes.
In the International System of Units, the derived unit of power; the power of a system in which one joule of energy is transferred per second. Symbol: W
Having a disposition to do good.
definitions.example: Chinese and Eastern mythologies describe dragons as benevolent.

definitions.definition: Possessing or manifesting love for mankind.
Altruistic, charitable, good, just and fair.
To confuse the sight of by means of excessive brightness.
definitions.example: Dazzled by the headlights of the lorry, the deer stopped in the middle of the street.

definitions.definition: To render incapable of thinking clearly; to overwhelm with showiness or brilliance.
definitions.example: The delegates were dazzled by the originality of his arguments.

definitions.definition: To be overpowered by light; to be confused by excess of brightness.
The action of the verb to dazzle; dazzlement
Shining intensely.
Splendid; brilliant
Superlative; astounding
A specimen of the now extinct species Homo neanderthalensis.
A primitive person.
Of or pertaining to Homo neanderthalensis.
definitions.example: The capacity of the Neanderthal skull was 10% larger than that of modern humans.

definitions.definition: Old-fashioned, opposed to change (in allusion to Homo neanderthalensis).
Of or pertaining to the Neander Valley in Germany.
To pack or bundle something.
To travel on a package holiday.
To prepare (a book, a television series, etc.), including all stages from research to production, in order to sell the result to a publisher or broadcaster.
Having been placed in a package.
Any of various neotropical frugivorous birds from the family Ramphastidae, with a large colorful beak.
Unconscious mind
Not awake; having no awareness.
definitions.example: After the anesthetist administered the general anesthetic the patient was unconscious.

definitions.definition: Without directed thought or awareness.
definitions.example: My sudden fright was an unconscious response.

definitions.definition: Engaged in skilled performance without conscious control.
Non-verbal communication by means of gestures, and/or other body movement.
Any activity designed to develop or hone a skill or ability.
definitions.example: The teacher told us that the next exercise is to write an essay.

definitions.definition: Activity intended to improve physical, or sometimes mental, strength and fitness.
definitions.example: I do crosswords for mental exercise.

definitions.definition: A setting in action or practicing; employment in the proper mode of activity; exertion; application; use.
definitions.example: The law guarantees us the free exercise of our rights.

definitions.definition: The performance of an office, ceremony, or duty.
definitions.example: I assisted the ailing vicar in the exercise of his parish duties.

definitions.definition: That which gives practice; a trial; a test.
To exert for the sake of training or improvement; to practice in order to develop.
definitions.example: to exercise troops or horses;  to exercise one's brain with a puzzle

definitions.definition: To perform physical activity for health or training.
definitions.example: I exercise at the gym every day.

definitions.definition: To use (a right, an option, etc.); to put into practice.
definitions.example: She is going to exercise her right to vote.

definitions.definition: (now often in passive) To occupy the attention and effort of; to task; to tax, especially in a painful or vexatious manner; harass; to vex; to worry or make anxious.
definitions.example: exercised with pain

definitions.definition: To set in action; to cause to act, move, or make exertion; to give employment to.
A thick, creamy soup or stew.
A stew, particularly fish or seafood, not necessarily thickened.
A seller of fish.
To make (seafood, etc.) into chowder.
To pour water into the soil surrounding (plants).
To wet or supply with water; to moisten; to overflow with water; to irrigate.
To provide (animals) with water for drinking.
definitions.example: I need to go water the cattle.

definitions.definition: To get or take in water.
definitions.example: The ship put into port to water.

definitions.definition: To urinate onto.
To dilute.
definitions.example: Can you water the whisky, please?

definitions.definition: To overvalue (securities), especially through deceptive accounting.
To fill with or secrete water.
definitions.example: Chopping onions makes my eyes water.

definitions.definition: To wet and calender, as cloth, so as to impart to it a lustrous appearance in wavy lines; to diversify with wavelike lines.
definitions.example: to water silk

definitions.definition: An act of watering, i.e. pouring water on something, or diluting a liquid.
definitions.example: The plants receive regular waterings.

definitions.definition: The art or process of giving a wavy, ornamental appearance to something.
The property of being versatile or having many different abilities; flexibility.
(by extension) Of speech or style: brief, concise, to the point.
(by extension) Of manner or speech: abruptly or brusquely short; curt.
Burnished, polished; fine, smooth; neat, spruce.
A shallow portion of an otherwise deep body of water.
definitions.example: The ship ran aground in an unexpected shallow.

definitions.definition: A fish, the rudd.
A costermonger's barrow.
To make or become less deep.
Having little depth; significantly less deep than wide.
definitions.example: Saute the onions in a shallow pan.

definitions.definition: Extending not far downward.
definitions.example: The water is shallow here.

definitions.definition: Concerned mainly with superficial matters.
definitions.example: It was a glamorous but shallow lifestyle.

definitions.definition: Lacking interest or substance.
definitions.example: The acting is good, but the characters are shallow.

definitions.definition: Not intellectually deep; not penetrating deeply; simple; not wise or knowing.
definitions.example: shallow learning

definitions.definition: Not deep in tone.
Not far forward, close to the net.
A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
Done by one side only.
Affecting only one side of the body.
Binding or affecting one party only.
One who kicks.
One who takes kicks.
The kicking strap.
An outboard motor.
An unexpected situation, detail or circumstance, often unpleasant.
definitions.example: John wants to climb the wall, but the kicker is that it is thirty feet tall.

definitions.definition: An enticement for investors, e.g. warranty added to the investment contract.
An unpaired card which is part of a pair, two pair, or three of a kind poker hand.
definitions.example: Jill's hand was two pair, aces and sevens, with a king kicker.

definitions.definition: Small text above a headline that indicates the topic of the story.
The last one or two paragraphs of a story.
A lighthearted or humorous item used to round off a news broadcast.
A device that periodically displaces a newspaper from the print production line, to aid in gathering the newspapers into fixed-size bundles.
A launch ramp.
The fermenting mass of fruit that is the basis of pruno, or "prison wine".
A relaxed party.
A backlight positioned at an angle.
An introduction; something that leads into the beginning of something.
definitions.example: After the long lead-in, the climax of the story was a disappointment.

definitions.definition: A region of data at the beginning of a compact disc, holding the table of contents.
A short phrase that begins the caption of a photograph.
A small Arctic and Subarctic rodent from any of six genera of similar rodents.
Any member of a group given to conformity or groupthink, especially a group poised to follow a leader off a cliff.
A special group or social class of people which have a superior intellectual, social or economic status as, the elite of society.
Someone who is among the best at a certain task.
Of high birth or social position; aristocratic or patrician.
Representing the choicest or most select of a group.
To set out the meaning of; to explain or discuss at length
To make a statement, especially at length.
definitions.example: He expounded often on the dangers of the imperial presidency.

definitions.definition: A trading establishment, especially set up by merchants working in a foreign country.
The position or state of being a factor.
A building or other place where manufacturing takes place.
A device which produces or manufactures something.
A factory farm.
definitions.example: chicken factory; pig factory

definitions.definition: In a computer program or library, a function, method, etc. which creates an object.
A police station.
(of a configuration, part, etc.) Having come from the factory in the state it is currently in; original, stock.
definitions.example: See how there's another layer of metal there? That's not factory.

definitions.definition: To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
definitions.example: They hacked the brush down and made their way through the jungle.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.
definitions.example: This cold is awful. I can't stop hacking.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.
definitions.example: Can you hack it out here with no electricity or running water?

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.
definitions.example: He went to the batter's box hacking.

definitions.definition: (soccer and rugby) To kick (a player) on the shins.
To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
To lay (bricks) on a rack to dry.
To keep (young hawks) in a state of partial freedom, before they are trained.
To make common or cliched; to vulgarise.
To ride a horse at a regular pace; to ride on a road (as opposed to riding cross-country etc.).
To be exposed or offered or to common use for hire; to turn prostitute.
To live the life of a drudge or hack.
To use as a hack; to let out for hire.
To use frequently and indiscriminately, so as to render trite and commonplace.
To play hackeysack.
Short and interrupted, broken, jerky; hacky.
definitions.example: A hacking cough. A hacking laugh. A hacking breath. A hacking cry.

definitions.definition: A bitless headpiece of rope or straps, placed on the head of animals such as cattle or horses to lead or tie them.
A rope with a noose, for hanging criminals; the gallows rope.
A halter top.
To place a halter on.
definitions.example: What do you mean, you didn't halter the horses when we stopped for the night?

definitions.definition: Eager or inordinate desire for some object that confers distinction, as preferment, honor, superiority, political power, or literary fame; desire to distinguish one's self from other people.
definitions.example: My son, John, wants to be a firefighter very much. He has a lot of ambition.

definitions.definition: An object of an ardent desire.
definitions.example: My ambition is to own a helicopter.

definitions.definition: A desire, as in (sense 1), for another person to achieve these things.
A personal quality similar to motivation, not necessarily tied to a single goal.
The act of going about to solicit or obtain an office, or any other object of desire; canvassing.
That part of the beard which grows upon the sides of the face, usually of the male, or upon the chin, or upon both.
A hair of the beard.
One of the long, projecting hairs growing at the sides of the mouth of a cat, or other animal.
The (very small) distance between two things.
definitions.example: It missed falling on your foot by a whisker.

definitions.definition: Spreaders from the bows to spread the bowsprit shrouds.
A graphic element that shows the maxima and minima in a box plot.
One who, or that which, whisks, or moves with a quick, sweeping motion.
A small tendril that forms on metal.
The agent noun of to walk: a person who walks or a thing which walks, especially a pedestrian or a participant in a walking race.
A walking frame.
(often in the plural) A shoe designed for comfortable walking.
A zombie.
A male escort who accompanies a woman to an event.
A gressorial bird.
A forester.
A kind of military robot or mecha with legs for locomotion.
A domestic animal, Equus asinus asinus, similar to a horse
A stubborn person
A fool
A small auxiliary engine
A bad poker player
Seemingly or apparently valid, likely, or acceptable; conceivably true or likely

definitions.definition: Obtaining approbation; specifically pleasing; apparently right; specious.
definitions.example: a plausible pretext; plausible manners; a plausible delusion

definitions.definition: Worthy of being applauded; praiseworthy; commendable; ready.
A basin used for holding water for washing.
A drain for carrying off wastewater.
A sinkhole.
A depression in land where water collects, with no visible outlet.
A heat sink.
A place that absorbs resources or energy.
The motion of a sinker pitch.
definitions.example: Jones has a two-seamer with heavy sink.

definitions.definition: An object or callback that captures events; event sink
A destination vertex in a transportation network
An abode of degraded persons; a wretched place.
A depression in a stereotype plate.
A stage trap-door for shifting scenery.
An excavation less than a shaft.
(game development) One or several systems that remove currency from the game's economy, thus controlling or preventing inflation
(heading, physical) To move or be moved into something.
(heading, social) To diminish or be diminished.
To conceal and appropriate.
To keep out of sight; to suppress; to ignore.
To pay absolutely.
definitions.example: I have sunk thousands of pounds into this project.

definitions.definition: To reduce or extinguish by payment.
definitions.example: to sink the national debt

definitions.definition: To be overwhelmed or depressed; to fail in strength.
To decrease in volume, as a river; to subside; to become diminished in volume or in apparent height.
Any of many swimming, often edible crustaceans, chiefly of the infraorder Caridea or the suborder Dendrobranchiata, with slender legs, long whiskers and a long abdomen.
The flesh of such crustaceans.
A small, puny or unimportant person.
To fish for shrimp.
To contract; to shrink.
The act of saying sorry; an apology.
(of a person) Regretful for an action; grieved or saddened, especially by the loss of something or someone.
definitions.example: I am sorry I stepped on your toes. It was an accident.

definitions.definition: Poor, pitifully sad or regrettable.
definitions.example: The storm left his garden in a sorry state.

definitions.definition: Pathetic and inferior to the point of causing others disgust.
definitions.example: Bob is a sorry excuse for a football player.

definitions.definition: Expresses regret, remorse, or sorrow.
definitions.example: Sorry! I didn't see that you were on the phone.

definitions.definition: Used as a request for someone to repeat something not heard or understood clearly.
definitions.example: Sorry? What was that? The phone cut out.

definitions.definition: Used to correct oneself in speech.
definitions.example: There are four — sorry, five — branches of the store locally.

definitions.definition: A disk, usually made of a polymer, used to record sound for playback on a phonograph.
An item of information put into a temporary or permanent physical medium.
definitions.example: The person had a record of the interview so she could review her notes.

definitions.definition: Any instance of a physical medium on which information was put for the purpose of preserving it and making it available for future reference.
definitions.example: We have no record of you making this payment to us.

definitions.definition: A set of data relating to a single individual or item.
The most extreme known value of some variable, particularly that of an achievement in competitive events.
definitions.example: The heat and humidity were both new records.

definitions.definition: To make a record of information.
definitions.example: I wanted to record every detail of what happened, for the benefit of future generations.

definitions.definition: To make an audio or video recording of.
definitions.example: Within a week they had recorded both the song and the video for it.

definitions.definition: To give legal status to by making an official public record.
definitions.example: When the deed was recorded, we officially owned the house.

definitions.definition: To fix in a medium, usually in a tangible medium.
To make an audio, video, or multimedia recording.
To repeat; to practice.
To sing or repeat a tune.
To reflect; to ponder.
Having no stain or blemish; spotless, undefiled, clear, clean, pure.
definitions.example: Thou sheer, immaculate and silver fountain. — Shakespeare, Richard II, V-iii.

definitions.definition: Lacking spots, blotches, or other markings; spotless; unspotted.
A tool with which one grates, especially cheese, to facilitate getting small particles or shreds off a solid lump
definitions.example: I'm making coleslaw; do you know where the grater is?

definitions.definition: A person who jogs (as exercise).
A tracksuit, particularly the trousers.
A printing press operator who removes, jogs, and stacks the sheets or signatures of paper.
(chiefly derogatory) A black person.

A method of self-defence established in Japan emphasizing “jū” (soft or gentle) “jutsu” (art or technique). Japanese martial art that utilizes a large variety of techniques in defense against an opponent.
Brazilian jiu-jitsu, a related art, derived from judo.
Precision maneuvering.
A glowing piece of coal or wood.
Smoldering ash.
A branching, hair-like structure that grows on the bodies of birds, used for flight, swimming, protection and display.
Long hair on the lower legs of a dog or horse, especially a draft horse, notably the Clydesdale breed. Narrowly only the rear hair.
One of the fins or wings on the shaft of an arrow.
A longitudinal strip projecting from an object to strengthen it, or to enter a channel in another object and thereby prevent displacement sideways but permit motion lengthwise; a spline.
Kind; nature; species (from the proverbial phrase "birds of a feather").

One of the two shims of the three-piece stone-splitting tool known as plug and feather or plug and feathers; the feathers are placed in a borehole and then a wedge is driven between them, causing the stone to split.
The angular adjustment of an oar or paddle-wheel float, with reference to a horizontal axis, as it leaves or enters the water.
Anything petty or trifling; a whit or jot.
(in the plural) Partridges and pheasants, as opposed to rabbits and hares (called fur).
To cover or furnish with feathers.
To arrange in the manner or appearance of feathers.
definitions.example: The stylist feathered my hair.

definitions.definition: To rotate the oars while they are out of the water to reduce wind resistance.
To streamline the blades of an aircraft's propeller by rotating them perpendicular to the axis of the propeller when the engine is shut down so that the propeller does not windmill during flight.
definitions.example: After striking the bird, the pilot feathered the left, damaged engine’s propeller.

definitions.definition: To finely shave or bevel an edge.
To intergrade or blend the pixels of an image with those of a background or neighboring image.
To adorn, as with feathers; to fringe.
To render light as a feather; to give wings to.
To enrich; to exalt; to benefit.
To tread, as a cock.
To accidentally touch the cue ball with the tip of the cue when taking aim
A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.
definitions.example: We've just received an urgent message from the President.

definitions.definition: An underlying theme or conclusion to be drawn from something.
definitions.example: The main message of the novel is that time heals all wounds.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) An errand.
See messages.
To send a message to; to transmit a message to, e.g. as text via a cell phone.
definitions.example: I messaged her about the concert.

definitions.definition: To send (something) as a message; usually refers to electronic messaging.
definitions.example: Please message the final report by fax.

definitions.definition: To send a message or messages; to be capable of sending messages.
definitions.example: The runaway computer program was messaging non-stop.

definitions.definition: To bear as a message.
A 42.195 kilometre (26 mile 385 yard) road race.
(by extension) Any extended or sustained activity.
definitions.example: He had a cleaning marathon the night before his girlfriend came over.

definitions.definition: To run a marathon.
To watch or read a large number of instalments of (a film, book, TV series, etc.) in one sitting.
definitions.example: We're going to marathon Star Trek next weekend.

definitions.definition: The part of a missile, projectile, torpedo, rocket, or other munition which contains either the nuclear or thermonuclear system, high explosive system, chemical or biological agents, or inert materials intended to inflict damage.
The glans penis.
A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .
A nonplus .
The white spot in the centre of a target; hence the object to which anything is directed or aimed, the range of such aim .
A lot by which nothing is gained; a ticket in a lottery on which no prize is indicated .
An empty space; a void, for example on a paper .

(now chiefly U.S.) A document, paper, or form with spaces left blank to be filled up at the pleasure of the person to whom it is given (e.g. a blank charter, ballot, form, contract, etc.), or as the event may determine; a blank form .
Blank verse .
A piece of metal (such as a coin, screw, nuts), cut and shaped to the required size of the thing to be made, and ready for the finishing operations; (coining) the disc of metal before stamping .
A vacant space, place, or period; a void .
The 1 / 230400 of a grain .
An empty space in one's memory; a forgotten item or memory .
A dash written in place of an omitted letter or word
The space character; the character resulting from pressing the space-bar on a keyboard.
A domino without points on one or both of its divisions.
definitions.example: the double blank

definitions.definition: Short for blank cartridge. .
(in the expression ‘shooting blanks’) An ineffective effort which achieves nothing .
To make void; to erase.
definitions.example: I blanked out my previous entry.

definitions.definition: To ignore (a person) deliberately.
definitions.example: She blanked me for no reason.

definitions.definition: To prevent from scoring, for example in a sporting event.
definitions.example: England blanks Wales to advance to the final.

definitions.definition: To become blank.
To be temporarily unable to remember.
definitions.example: I'm blanking on her name right now.

definitions.definition: An archeological or paleontological investigation, or the site where such an investigation is taking place.
A plodding and laborious student.
A thrust; a poke.

definitions.definition: A tool for digging.

A cutting, sarcastic remark.

definitions.definition: To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel. Or to drill, or the like, through rocks, roads, or the like. More generally, to make any similar hole by moving material out of the way.
definitions.example: If the plane can't pull out of the dive it is in, it'll dig a hole in the ground.

definitions.definition: To get by digging; to take from the ground; often with up.

definitions.definition: To take ore from its bed, in distinction from making excavations in search of ore.
To work like a digger; to study ploddingly and laboriously.
To investigate, to research, often followed by out or up.

definitions.definition: To thrust; to poke.

definitions.definition: To defend against an attack hit by the opposing team by successfully passing the ball
To understand or show interest in.

definitions.definition: To appreciate, or like.

Clothes.

A small, young bird that is still confined to the nest.
A nest; a receptacle.

A position in which the player on the move must yield with his king allowing his opponent to advance with his own king.
The difference of quantity or quality between two propositions having the same subject and predicate.
An educational institution for young children, usually between ages 4 and 6; nursery school.

The two levels between nursery and prep; the second and third years of preschool.
Makeup for the lips.

A product of equal factors (and generalizations of this notion): x^n, read as "x to the power of n" or the like, is called a power and denotes the product x \times x \times \cdots \times x, where x appears n times in the product; x is called the base and n the exponent.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
